name: Zaos Reservoir Circular Trail
name_original: Embalse del Zaos – run circular desde Oliva de la Frontera
description: This moderate 4.6-mile (7.4 km) jogging route around Embalse del Zaos offers tranquil views and accessible paths near Oliva de la Frontera.
region_intro: Jogging routes around Oliva de la Frontera traverse a diverse natural environment in the province of Badajoz, Extremadura, Spain. The landscape features expansive dehesas, characterized by ancient oak trees and extensive pastures, alongside rolling hills. The region is also influenced by the Ardila River, which forms a natural border and offers serene riparian environments. These varied terrains provide numerous scenic routes for runners, from gentle paths through open lands to more challenging trails in hilly areas. Best jogging routes around Oliva De La Frontera The most popular jogging route is Zaos Reservoir Circular Trail, a 4.6 miles (7.4 km) trail that takes 52 minutes 33 seconds to complete. This moderate route offers views around the Embalse del Zaos. Another top favourite among local runners is Boticas loop from Monte da Coitadinha, a difficult 11.2 miles (18.1 km) path. This route features significant elevation gain, making it suitable for experienced runners seeking a challenge. Local runners also love the Colorada – Miradouro de Barrancos loop from Barrancos, a 7.6 miles (12.3 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 1 hour 33 minutes. Jogging around Oliva De La Frontera is defined by dehesa landscapes, riverine paths, and rolling hills. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate runs to more difficult trails. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 30 runners have used komoot to explore Oliva De La Frontera's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Oliva de la Frontera, and what are their difficulty levels?
A: There are over 6 running routes around Oliva de la Frontera. These include 3 moderate routes, perfect for a steady run, and 3 difficult routes for those seeking a more challenging experience with significant elevation changes.

Q: Are there any circular running routes around Oliva de la Frontera?
A: Yes, the region offers several circular running routes. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991762464/zaos-reservoir-circular-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zaos Reservoir Circular Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 4.6-mile (7.4 km) loop offering picturesque views around the Embalse del Zaos.

Q: What kind of scenic views can I expect while running in Oliva de la Frontera?
A: Runners in Oliva de la Frontera can enjoy diverse and beautiful scenery. Expect to traverse expansive &lt;strong&gt;dehesas&lt;/strong&gt; with ancient oak trees, follow serene paths along the &lt;strong&gt;Ardila River&lt;/strong&gt;, and take in views around the &lt;strong&gt;Embalse del Zaos&lt;/strong&gt;. For panoramic vistas, consider routes that pass by viewpoints like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5713982" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View from Noudar Castle&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I expect to see any wildlife while jogging in the region?
A: Absolutely. Oliva de la Frontera is recognized as an Important Bird Area in Europe. While running through the natural spaces, you might spot over a hundred species of birds, including griffon vultures, black vultures, common buzzards, and black kites.

Q: Are there any challenging running paths for experienced runners?
A: Yes, for experienced runners seeking a challenge, there are difficult routes available. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32127127" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Boticas loop from Monte da Coitadinha&lt;/a&gt; is an 11.2-mile (18.1 km) path with significant elevation gain, ideal for a demanding run.

Q: What types of terrain will I encounter on the running trails?
A: The running trails around Oliva de la Frontera feature a variety of terrain. You'll find paths through open, gently undulating &lt;strong&gt;dehesas&lt;/strong&gt;, serene routes along riverbanks, and more challenging trails in the mountainous areas characterized by rolling hills and jara shrubs.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural sights I can incorporate into my run?
A: Yes, you can combine your run with cultural exploration. The region has several historical sites that can serve as scenic markers. The Ermita de San Isidro Labrador, located in the Dehesa Boyal, offers a cultural destination. Other notable attractions include the Parish Church of San Marcos and the Sanctuary of Nuestra Señora de Gracia, which are often part of urban or peri-urban runs.

Q: What is a good running route for someone looking for a moderate distance?
A: For a moderate distance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991762464/zaos-reservoir-circular-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zaos Reservoir Circular Trail&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent choice. It's 4.6 miles (7.4 km) long and offers beautiful views around the reservoir, making it suitable for most fitness levels.

Q: Are there any running routes that follow the Ardila River?
A: The Ardila River forms a natural border with Portugal and runs through impressively beautiful mountainous landscapes. Its riverbanks offer charming routes that are suitable for jogging, allowing runners to experience a serene riparian environment.

Q: What makes the dehesa landscape unique for running in this area?
A: Oliva de la Frontera is situated within one of the world's largest dehesa areas. This landscape, dominated by ancient oak trees and extensive pastures, provides a picturesque and tranquil setting for running. The open, gently undulating lands offer varied and enjoyable routes, unique to this part of Extremadura.

Q: Is there a longer, more varied running route available?
A: For a longer and varied experience, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38167032" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Colorada – Miradouro de Barrancos loop from Barrancos&lt;/a&gt; is a 7.6-mile (12.3 km) trail. This route leads through diverse terrain and is often completed in about 1 hour 33 minutes, offering a good challenge and changing scenery.

Q: Are there any routes that offer views of the Embalse del Zaos?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991762464/zaos-reservoir-circular-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zaos Reservoir Circular Trail&lt;/a&gt; specifically offers views around the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2799550" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zaos Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. This area is a popular spot for outdoor activities and provides picturesque views during your run.
tips: Good picnic area next to the reservoir. You can enjoy a pleasant environment in the Zaos Reservoir where, in addition to enjoying its landscape, it is possible to practice fishing or canoeing.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Embalse del Zaos circular run?
faqs.answer: The trail is easily accessible from the town of Oliva de la Frontera. While specific parking areas are not detailed, you can typically find parking near the reservoir itself or in Oliva de la Frontera, which serves as a convenient starting point for this route.

faqs.question: Is the Embalse del Zaos circular run suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Yes, this trail is generally considered easy and suitable for all fitness levels, including beginners. It features easily accessible paths and a moderate elevation gain, making it ideal for a relaxed jog.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this circular route?
faqs.answer: The trail primarily features easily accessible paths, suggesting a relatively smooth and runnable surface. While specific details about dirt or gravel are not extensively mentioned, you can expect a comfortable jogging experience.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery will I see along the route?
faqs.answer: You can expect picturesque views surrounding the Zaos Reservoir. The area offers a pleasant and tranquil setting, with the reservoir providing a scenic backdrop. The region is also known for its 'dehesa' landscape (holm oak meadows and pasture), which changes colors with the seasons.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to run this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the 'dehesa' landscape and the climate of Extremadura, spring and autumn are generally the best times to visit. The weather is milder, and the natural surroundings are at their most vibrant or offer beautiful seasonal colors. Summers can be quite hot, making early mornings or late evenings preferable if running during that season.

faqs.question: Is the Embalse del Zaos circular run dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While no specific restrictions are mentioned, the Embalse del Zaos is a natural environment. It is generally understood that dogs are welcome, provided they are kept on a leash and owners practice responsible pet ownership by cleaning up after them. This helps protect the local flora and fauna.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there is no mention of any permits or entrance fees required to access the Embalse del Zaos circular run. It is a publicly accessible natural area.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this circular run?
faqs.answer: This circular route is approximately 7.4 kilometers long. With a moderate jogging pace, it can typically be completed in about 50-60 minutes. For a more relaxed pace or brisk walk, it might take up to two hours.

faqs.question: Are there any notable features or points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: The primary point of interest is the Zaos Reservoir itself, which offers beautiful views. The area also serves as a recreational hub with a picnic area, and you can enjoy the local flora and fauna within the natural environment, including holm oak meadows.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife can I expect to see around the Embalse del Zaos?
faqs.answer: The reservoir is situated within a natural environment, offering opportunities to enjoy local flora and fauna. While specific species are not listed, you might encounter various birds, small mammals, and insects typical of the 'dehesa' landscape and aquatic environments in Extremadura.

faqs.question: Is public transport available to reach the Embalse del Zaos from Oliva de la Frontera?
faqs.answer: The trail is easily reachable from Oliva de la Frontera. However, specific public transport options directly to the reservoir are not detailed. It is generally recommended to travel by car or consider local taxi services from Oliva de la Frontera for convenient access.
